summary: FlexOfficers MVP fully tested: 22/22 backend pytest tests passed (auth, shifts, apply, applications, messages, role-permissions). Frontend mobile flows verified end-to-end: welcome → role-preselected register → browse with sticky header + filter chips → shift detail via View Shift → Apply Now (success toast + 'Applied' state) → My Shifts shows applied shift → Messages renders stub conversations → Profile shows user info + all 4 verification badges → Logout returns to welcome. Login with seeded test_officer@example.com / test1234 works. One minor deprecation warning observed (props.pointerEvents) from a third-party RN package. Shift card body itself is not tappable — only the 'View Shift' CTA navigates (PRD said tapping the card should work, but the CTA is prominent so it is acceptable UX). Backend includes idempotent apply (already_applied flag), proper 401/403/400/404 status codes, JWT auth, and bcrypt password hashing.

ui_bugs.issue: Card body (View wrapper) is not tappable; only the inner 'View Shift' TouchableOpacity navigates. PRD describes 'tapping a shift card navigates to detail'. Consider wrapping the entire card in a Pressable/TouchableOpacity for better mobile UX.

react_native_issues.issue: Deprecation warning in browser console: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Originates from a dependency, not app code. Update affected library when an upgrade is available.

action_items: (Optional UX) Make the entire ShiftCard tappable in /app/frontend/src/components/ShiftCard.tsx by wrapping the root View in a Pressable/TouchableOpacity that also calls onPress.
(Optional) Investigate which dependency is emitting the props.pointerEvents deprecation warning and upgrade when a fix is released.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') — consider migrating to lifespan context manager in a future iteration.

logger is defined after seed_data() references it (lines 445 uses logger before logger is assigned at line 483). Currently works because seed_data is awaited at startup AFTER module-level execution finishes, but it's a code-smell — move logging.basicConfig() and logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) above SEED_SHIFTS/seed_data definition.
Backend uses Pydantic v1 syntax body.dict() — fine for now, but plan migration to body.model_dump() when on Pydantic v2.
seed_data_creation: Each backend test run registers two TEST_-prefixed users (officer + company) with unique UUID-suffixed emails. One TEST_Patrol shift is created by the company in test scope. No cleanup performed (test data uses TEST_ prefix for easy identification).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend URL is in EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL (not EXPO_BACKEND_URL) in frontend/.env. Pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_flexofficers_api.py covers auth, shifts CRUD, apply, applications, messages with role-permission edge cases. Frontend testIDs: welcome (role-officer-card, role-company-card, welcome-login-link); register (register-{name,email,password,company,submit,error}-input/button, role-tab-{officer,company}); login (login-{email,password,submit}-input/button); browse (filter-chip-{all,open,filling_fast}, shift-card-{n}, shift-card-{n}-view-button, notifications-button); shift detail (shift-apply-button, shift-detail-back, shift-toast); profile (profile-logout-button). Tab labels for Browse/My Shifts/Messages/Profile are accessible via text locator. Apply toast 'Application sent!' shows for 2.2s then disappears — wait <2s after Apply click to capture it.
